facts.text: The Bellmore Historical Association, formed in 2015, partners the Chamber of Commerce, the Bellmore Memorial Library, and the North Bellmore Public Library.
North Bellmore’s roots reach back to 1643, when the area was a quiet farming and fishing community until the late 20th century.
Downtown Bellmore houses the historic Bellmore Movies & Showplace, a theater that has entertained locals for decades.
The community boasts two Long Island Rail Road stations—Bellmore Station and the historical North Bellmore Station—providing quick access to New York City.
Bellmore Station offers frequent service that links residents to both Manhattan and Long Island destinations.
North Bellmore and Bellmore together are collectively called “The Bellmores,” reflecting their shared history and community spirit.
